wiki/communities/cli-commands.md.title: CLI Commands
wiki/communities/cli-commands.md.purpose: Documents all 19 CLI subcommand handlers: what each command does, its flags, and the DB/service calls it makes. The reference for adding new commands or tracing what happens when a user runs mimirs <subcommand>.

sections.title: Per-file breakdown
sections.purpose: Give each load-bearing file its own prose section — role, key exports, flags accepted, and the non-obvious behavior a reader would miss from signatures alone.

sections.title: How it works
sections.purpose: Trace the runtime flow from CLI entry (cli/index.ts) through argument dispatch to a concrete command handler and its DB calls.

sections.title: Dependencies and consumers
sections.purpose: Show who this community imports from and who imports it, at a glance.

sections.title: Internals
sections.purpose: Surface the insider knowledge that's not obvious from signatures — shared setup patterns, session-context helper, flag parsing conventions.

sections.title: Why it's built this way
sections.purpose: Explain non-obvious structural decisions and the alternatives rejected.

sections.title: Trade-offs
sections.purpose: Name the gains and costs of the current design so a future maintainer can tell whether a constraint is load-bearing.

sections.title: Common gotchas
sections.purpose: Things a first-time reader of this community will get wrong.

wiki/communities/wiki-pipeline-internals.title: Wiki Pipeline — Types & Internals
wiki/communities/wiki-pipeline-internals.purpose: Documents the data model, algorithms, and helper modules that power the wiki generation pipeline: type contracts, PageRank scoring, community bundling, staleness detection, page tree construction, and semantic query templates. Read this before touching any wiki generation step.

wiki/communities/wiki-orchestrator.md.title: Wiki Orchestrator & MCP Tools
wiki/communities/wiki-orchestrator.md.purpose: Documents the wiki orchestration layer: the generate_wiki MCP tool handlers, the wiki/index.ts barrel, the lint-page validator, the section catalog registry, and the update log writer. Read this to understand how a generate_wiki() call becomes a page payload.

wiki/communities/indexing-pipeline.md.title: Indexing Pipeline
wiki/communities/indexing-pipeline.md.purpose: Documents the file ingestion pipeline: directory validation, file parsing, AST-aware chunking, embedding, and DB upsert. The authoritative reference for adding new language support or tuning chunk behavior.

wiki/communities/search-runtime.md.title: Search Runtime
wiki/communities/search-runtime.md.purpose: Documents the hybrid search engine: vector + FTS merging, ranking boosts, usage lookup helpers, and the benchmark/eval harness used to measure retrieval quality. The central reference for anyone tuning search relevance.

wiki/communities/conversation-server.md.title: Conversation Indexer & MCP Server
wiki/communities/conversation-server.md.purpose: Documents the JSONL conversation parsing and indexing pipeline, plus the MCP server bootstrap that wires all tool groups together. The reference for understanding how Claude Code session history is ingested and how the server starts.

wiki/communities/community-detection.md.title: Community Detection & Discovery
wiki/communities/community-detection.md.purpose: Documents the Louvain-based community detection and the discovery orchestrator that feeds it: how the file-level import graph is extracted, how communities are assigned, and how the DiscoveryResult is built for downstream wiki stages.

wiki/communities/config-embeddings.md.title: Config & Embeddings
wiki/communities/config-embeddings.md.purpose: Documents the embedding singleton and config loader: how the local transformer model is initialized, how batch embedding works, and how the YAML config file overrides model and dimension defaults. The reference before touching anything that calls embed() or loadConfig().

wiki/communities/graph-watcher.md.title: Import Graph & File Watcher
wiki/communities/graph-watcher.md.purpose: Documents the static import graph resolver and the filesystem watcher: how inter-file dependencies are extracted and persisted, and how file-change events trigger incremental re-indexing. The reference for understanding dependency tracking and live-index updates.

wiki/communities/cli-setup.md.purpose: Documents the first-run setup module: how mimirs writes MCP config files for supported IDEs, injects .gitignore entries, detects agent hints, and ensures a valid config file exists. The reference for adding a new IDE target or debugging init failures.

The thin adapter between the MCP protocol and the search-runtime community.

wiki/communities/wiki-pipeline-internals.title: src/db/types.ts
wiki/communities/wiki-pipeline-internals.purpose: Per-file detail for `src/db/types.ts` — a member of the Wiki Pipeline — Types & Internals community.

sections.title: Role
sections.purpose: 1-2 sentences naming the file's role in the parent community: what concern it owns, what other members it leans on.

sections.title: Exports
sections.purpose: Every export from this file with signature + 1-sentence description of what it does (not just types). Quote constants verbatim (e.g. `DEFAULT_WEIGHT = 0.7`).
sections.shape: markdown table: Name | Kind | Signature | What it does

sections.title: Internals
sections.purpose: Non-obvious behavior a reader would miss from signatures alone — silent fallbacks, tuning knobs disguised as constants, defensive guards, heuristics with blind spots. Skip if the file is pure signature glue.
sections.shape: bulleted list: bold claim, then 1-3 sentences of elaboration with file:line references

wiki/architecture.md.title: Architecture
wiki/architecture.md.purpose: Bird's-eye view of how the codebase is organised — communities, their relationships, and the load-bearing files that anchor everything.

sections.title: System map
sections.purpose: Mermaid diagram showing how communities relate to each other at a high level

sections.title: Load-bearing files
sections.purpose: Short list of the top PageRank files and what they anchor

sections.title: Entry points
sections.purpose: Files with no incoming edges — the graph sinks / true entry points

sections.title: Cross-cutting dependencies
sections.purpose: Communities or modules that everything depends on, pulled out of the main map

sections.title: Design decisions
sections.purpose: 3–6 numbered structural choices that shaped the codebase — each one paragraph: the decision, the alternative it beat, and the reason. Source from architectural annotations, the top-community `design-rationale` sections, and root docs (README, ARCHITECTURE, ADRs). Do not fabricate — skip the section only if the bundle truly has no material.
sections.shape: numbered list of bold decision titles, each followed by a short paragraph (decision → alternative → reason)

wiki/data-flows.md.title: Data flows
wiki/data-flows.md.purpose: How a request travels end-to-end for each of the project's primary triggers. Pairs with the architecture page: architecture shows where things live, this shows what runs in what order.

sections.title: Overview
sections.purpose: One paragraph naming the flows and where each is triggered (CLI command, HTTP handler, library entry, watcher event).

sections.title: Flow 1
sections.purpose: Pick the most-run flow. Start with a `sequenceDiagram` tracing the call path across communities, then a numbered step list citing the specific files/functions. Include an `### Error paths` subsection when the flow has real error handling (retries, fallbacks, graceful degradation).

sections.title: Flow 2
sections.purpose: Second most-used flow, same shape as Flow 1. Prefer sequence diagrams; only fall back to flowchart if the flow is a pipeline with no caller/callee relationship.

sections.title: Additional flows
sections.purpose: If the codebase has a third or fourth named flow (e.g. background sync, incremental update, pre-flight validation), cover it here. Omit the section entirely when there are no additional flows — do not stub.

wiki/getting-started.md.title: Getting started
wiki/getting-started.md.purpose: Fastest path from zero to a working install. Points onward into the architecture page and the most-used community.

sections.title: What this is
sections.purpose: One-paragraph summary of the project from the README

sections.title: Installation
sections.purpose: How to install dependencies and build

sections.title: First run
sections.purpose: The shortest path to seeing something work — command, expected output

sections.title: Where to look next
